steps.instruction: Crush Szechuan peppercorns and black peppercorns in a mortar until medium-fine — you want texture, not powder.
Slice squid tubes into rings. Coat with flaky salt and the crushed pepper mixture.
Mix plain flour and cornflour together on a flat plate.
Beat the egg in a bowl. Toss squid rings in the egg to coat.
Coat squid rings in the flour mix in batches. Shake off any excess and place on a tray so they don't stick together.
Heat oil to 180°C. Fry in four batches for about 3 minutes until golden and crispy. Drain on a rack.
In a hot pan, add olive oil and garlic. Once it sizzles, toss in the fried squid and parsley. Toss for 30 seconds.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges.
Peel and chop onion. Peel garlic cloves. Wash and chop leek.
Heat olive oil in a heavy pot over medium heat. Add onion, leek and dried thyme. Sweat gently — do not brown. Add garlic and turn to low. Cook gently for 15 minutes.
Peel and dice potatoes. Add to the pot with all the chicken stock. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer gently for 25–30 minutes.
Add cream and blend completely smooth using a stick blender or regular blender. Taste and adjust seasoning.
For croutons, dice bread and fry in olive oil on low heat until brown and crispy — slow frying keeps them crispy in the soup.
Serve soup in bowls with croutons and chopped parsley on top.

name: Sheet Pan Greek Chicken
name_tl: Greek na Manok sa Sheet Pan
name_il: Manok a Greek iti Sheet Pan

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 220°C with your biggest tray inside — it should be very hot before the food goes in.
Zest and juice both lemons. Whisk with olive oil, garlic, oregano, Dijon, smoked paprika, salt and pepper to make the dressing. Keep one-third aside for the vegetables.
Toss only the chicken and potatoes in two-thirds of the dressing. Arrange on the hot tray, chicken skin-side up and potatoes cut-side down. Bake 20–25 minutes until potatoes have browned edges.
While that roasts, toss the red onion, capsicum, broccoli, cherry tomatoes and olives in the remaining dressing.
Remove tray from oven. Scatter the vegetables into the gaps around the chicken. Return to oven for 30–35 more minutes until chicken is golden and reaches 75°C inside.
Remove from oven. Scatter feta over everything while still hot. Finish with parsley and dill before serving.

name: Red Lentil Soup with Garlic Sourdough Croutons
name_tl: Sopas ng Pulang Lentil na may Croutons
name_il: Sopas ti Nalabbaga nga Lentil ken Croutons

steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and cook until soft and lightly golden, about 6 minutes.
Add cumin and black pepper. Stir for 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add grated carrot and potato. Stir for 1 minute to coat in spices.
Add rinsed lentils and stock. Season with salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer for 18–20 minutes until lentils are very soft.
For croutons: cube sourdough and fry in olive oil over medium heat until golden, 3–4 minutes. In the last 10 seconds, add minced garlic and toss through.
Blend soup completely smooth. Stir in sumac. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve in bowls topped with parsley, garlic croutons, a sprinkle of sumac and lemon halves to squeeze over.

ingredients.item: Dutch cream or Yukon Gold potatoes medium

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 220°C. Rub potatoes with olive oil and salt. Prick each potato a few times. Bake 55–65 minutes turning once until skins are dry and completely soft inside — a knife should slide in with zero resistance.
Cool 10–15 minutes. Slice off the top ¼ inch (save the skin to snack on). Scoop out flesh leaving a ¼ inch shell all around.
Pass potato flesh through a ricer into a saucepan. Do NOT use a blender.
Over medium-low heat, stir potato purée with butter, cream, salt, pepper and a little nutmeg until silky and smooth. Remove from heat and fold in chives. Taste — it should already be delicious.
In a small bowl mix the remaining cream, egg yolks, Gruyère, pepper and nutmeg into a loose savoury custard.
Spoon purée back into potato shells, mounding slightly. Use the back of a spoon to create a shallow channel down the centre. Carefully spoon the custard into the channel.
Bake at 180°C for 12–15 minutes until custard is just set and lightly golden. If not browned, grill briefly for 1–2 minutes — watch closely.

name: Spiced Indian Roast Potatoes
name_tl: Inihaw na Patatas na may Pampalasa ng India

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C. Place an empty roasting tray in the oven to preheat.
Boil potatoes in water with salt, turmeric and baking soda for 10 minutes until just tender. Drain in a colander and toss to roughen the edges. Sprinkle with cornstarch and toss again.

Heat oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add garlic and chili, fry until light golden. Add cumin and mustard seeds and let them crackle. Strain the oil into a bowl — keep the crispy garlic and spices aside.
Pour the spiced oil onto the hot roasting tray. Spread potatoes in a single layer and toss to coat. Roast 40 minutes, turning twice for even browning.
Once golden and crisp, remove from oven. Melt butter over the hot potatoes, drizzle with lemon juice, and season. Add the reserved crispy garlic and spices, then finish with mint and coriander.
Preheat oven to 180°C.
Combine cream, milk, nutmeg, salt and pepper in a jug.
Add minced garlic to a large saucepan and pour in the milk and cream mixture. Warm gently.
Peel and slice potatoes about 2mm thick using a mandoline or sharp knife. Add slices directly to the warm cream — do not rinse, the starch thickens the sauce.
Bring to a gentle simmer, stirring so the bottom doesn't catch. Simmer 8 minutes until just tender. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Transfer half the potatoes to a baking dish. Scatter thyme and a handful of cheese. Add remaining potatoes in a neat top layer. Pour over remaining liquid. Top with remaining cheese.
Cover loosely with foil. Bake 40 minutes. Remove foil and bake uncovered 25–30 more minutes until bubbling, golden and a knife slides through easily.
Rest covered with foil and a kitchen towel for 60 minutes — the cream sets and layers hold together. Potatoes stay hot.
Combine olive oil, harissa, hot honey, smoked paprika, lemon juice and salt. Add shrimp, toss to coat, and marinate 10–15 minutes.
For tabbouleh: mix lemon juice, olive oil, sumac and salt. Add bulgur, parsley, tomatoes, cucumber and red onion. Toss and set aside.
For tahini sauce: whisk tahini, lemon juice, cold water, garlic and cumin until smooth. Add more water if needed for drizzling consistency.
Heat a frying pan over medium-high. Cook shrimp 1–2 minutes per side until just opaque and lightly charred.
Warm flatbreads in a dry pan. Spread with tahini sauce, top with tabbouleh, then shrimp, thin cucumber slices and fresh dill.

name: Spanish Garlic Shrimp
name_il: Hipon ti Espanya nga adda Ahos

steps.instruction: In a frying pan over low heat, add olive oil with garlic slices and chili. Stew gently for 2 minutes until fragrant — do not brown the garlic or it turns bitter.
Add prawns in a single layer. Sprinkle with salt and smoked paprika. Cook 1–2 minutes each side until just opaque.
Add lemon juice and parsley. Toss and remove from heat immediately.
Serve sizzling hot straight from the pan with crusty bread for dunking.

name: Roasted Carrot and Ginger Soup
name_tl: Sopas ng Inihaw na Karot at Luya
name_il: Sopas ti Natuno nga Karot ken Laya

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C. Place carrots, onion and unpeeled garlic on a roasting tray. Drizzle with olive oil, season with salt and roast 25–30 minutes until tender and lightly caramelised.
Squeeze roasted garlic from its skin. Transfer all roasted vegetables to a large soup pot. Add ginger, turmeric, cumin, coriander, paprika, nutmeg and bay leaf.
Pour in stock and simmer gently for 25–30 minutes. Remove the bay leaf.
Blend until smooth and silky. Return to low heat and stir in coconut milk. Adjust seasoning. Add lemon juice for freshness if desired.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh coriander or parsley.
Make the dressing: crush garlic and anchovies to a paste. Add lemon juice, both vinegars, oregano and olive oil. Whisk well.
Boil potatoes in salted water until tender. Cool and peel.
Add 4 tablespoons of dressing to a large bowl. Add tomatoes, grated carrot, halved artichoke hearts, rocket and tuna. Slice potatoes and add them. Toss well.
Top with torn mozzarella, olives, a drizzle of olive oil and freshly torn basil. Serve with optional anchovies on the side.

name: Pan Seared Salmon with Lemon Butter Cream Sauce
name_tl: Piniritong Salmon na may Sarsa ng Limon at Mantikilya
name_il: Pinirito nga Salmon nga adda Sarsa ti Limon ken Mantikilia

steps.instruction: Remove skin from salmon if desired. Finely chop shallots and parsley leaves.
Melt butter in a frying pan over medium heat. Place salmon top-side down and cook until lightly golden. Flip gently and cook until butter turns nutty brown.
Deglaze with white wine, add lemon juice and shallots. Add cream. Season. Poach the fish in the sauce for about 3 minutes until cooked to your liking.
Remove salmon carefully and place on a warm plate. Reduce the sauce on high heat until thickened. Stir in parsley and pour over the fish.
Heat oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add chicken cubes and stir-fry 5–7 minutes until cooked through.
Add onion, white spring onion parts, garlic and ginger. Cook 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add capsicums, carrots and broccoli. Stir-fry 4–5 minutes until tender-crisp. Add a splash of water if needed to steam through.
Return chicken to pan. Add oyster sauce, soy sauce and chili sauce. Mix, then add cornstarch slurry (cornstarch + water). Cook 2–3 minutes until glossy.
Finish with sesame oil. Serve over rice or noodles. Garnish with spring onion greens and sesame seeds.

name: Italian Chicken Stuffed Peppers
name_tl: Pinalamanan ng Manok na Bell Pepper
name_il: Bell Pepper nga Napnuan iti Manok

ingredients.item: large bell peppers red or yellow

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C. Heat olive oil and butter in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add onion with a pinch of salt and cook until soft.
Add garlic and oregano. Add tomato paste and cook 1–2 minutes. Add chicken mince and break up, cooking until done with light browning.
Pour in passata. Simmer uncovered 12–15 minutes until thick. Remove from heat and stir in rice, Parmesan and parsley. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Cut peppers in half lengthwise through the stem. Remove seeds and ribs. Season inside with salt and pepper.
Spoon filling into pepper halves. Top with grated mozzarella. Pour 3 tbsp water into the baking dish base. Cover tightly with foil and bake 20 minutes.
Remove foil and bake uncovered 10–15 more minutes until peppers are tender and cheese is melted and golden.
Grate potatoes finely. Hand-squeeze out as much liquid as possible and place in a bowl.
Add finely sliced onion, crushed garlic, thyme, rosemary. Season with salt and pepper. Mix well.

Melt clarified butter. Heat a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat with the butter. Divide potato mixture into four portions. Press gently into the pan. Turn to low and cook 10–12 minutes until golden brown on the underside.
Flip and cook the other side 10–12 minutes until golden.
Cook bacon in a dry pan until crispy. Slice spring onions.
Plate rosti topped with goat yogurt, crispy bacon and spring onion.
Mix soft butter with minced garlic and chopped parsley to make garlic butter.
Flatten chicken thighs. Grill in a pan with olive oil, salt and pepper until cooked through.

Spread garlic butter on one side of each bread slice. Spread cream cheese on the other side. Layer Gruyère, cooked chicken pieces and more Gruyère. Top with second bread slice, garlic butter side out.
Place sandwiches garlic-butter-side down in a large frying pan on low heat under a lid. Grill until golden, 3–4 minutes per side. Flip and cook the other side.
Cut into triangles and serve hot.

name: Steamed Fish with Lemon Beurre Blanc
name_tl: Nilagang Isda na may Sarsa ng Limon at Mantikilya
name_il: Nabukbok nga Ikan nga adda Sarsa ti Limon ken Mantikilia

steps.instruction: Season fish with salt, pepper and a drizzle of olive oil.
Fill a pot with 2 inches of water and bring to a simmer. Position a steaming basket above the water. Place fish in the basket. Cover and steam 8–10 minutes until opaque and flaking.
While fish steams, combine white wine, lemon juice and shallots in a small saucepan. Simmer until reduced by half.
Reduce heat to low. Whisk in chilled butter cubes one at a time, whisking constantly to emulsify the sauce. Do not let it boil.
Stir in capers and dill. Season to taste. Spoon sauce generously over steamed fish. Serve immediately.

name: Pan-Fried Halloumi with Mediterranean Salad
name_tl: Piniritong Halloumi na may Ensaladang Mediteraneo
name_il: Pinirito nga Halloumi ken Ensalada ti Mediteraneo

steps.instruction: Make the dressing: whisk olive oil, lemon juice, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, grated garlic, salt and pepper in a large bowl.
Add tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, olives, capsicum, basil and parsley to the bowl. Toss well. Set aside some dressing for drizzling at the end.
Toast pine nuts in a dry pan over medium heat 1–2 minutes until golden. Set aside.
Pat halloumi dry with paper towel and slice into thick pieces.
Cook halloumi in a dry pan over medium heat 1–2 minutes per side to release moisture. Add a little olive oil and cook 30–60 seconds per side until golden and crisp.
Arrange salad on plates. Scatter pine nuts over top. Place warm halloumi on the salad, drizzle with remaining dressing. Serve immediately.

name: Potato Rosti with Smoked Salmon
name_tl: Patatas Rosti na may Smoked Salmon

steps.instruction: Steam potatoes for about 20 minutes until firm in the centre with soft edges. Cool slightly, then peel.
Grate potatoes using a coarse grater. Place in a bowl.
Add finely chopped shallot, crushed garlic, egg yolk, chopped thyme and rosemary. Season with salt and pepper. Add melted clarified butter. Mix together.
Heat a lightly oiled non-stick pan over medium heat. Use a cutter or mould to shape portions of the mixture. Cook until golden on the bottom, then flip and cook the other side until equally golden.
Serve rosti hot topped with sour cream, sliced avocado and smoked salmon.
